paper.title: 진동 기반 물질 전달 향상을 이용한 휴대형 망간 이온 센서
paper.authors: 최도은, 박주형, 박현준, 김치현, 채경환, 김가영, 박대일, 조원준, 장다영, 김수빈, 강채영, 허은슬, 박진성(성균관대)
paper.abstract: Manganese ions (Mn?*) are essential trace elements in biological systems; however, excessive exposure can cause neurological disorders and environmental contamination, particularly in drinking water. Therefore, the development of sensitive and portable detection systems for Mn?* monitoring is highly important. In this study, we developed a portable electrochemical sensor for manganese ion detection based on vibration-assisted signal enhancement. The electrode surface was sequentially modified by depositing graphite particles, followed by the electrochemical reduction of graphene oxide (GO) to improve electrical conductivity. Subsequently, a Nafion polymer layer was introduced as a cation-exchange membrane to enhance ion selectivity and preconcentration of Mn?*. The detection was performed using cathodic stripping voltammetry (CSV). During the deposition step of CSV, a coin vibrator was applied beneath the electrode to induce vibration, promoting mass transport and improving the accumulation efficiency of manganese ions on the electrode surface. As a result, the proposed sensor exhibited significantly enhanced electrochemical signals and achieved a limit of detection (LOD) of 489 pM. This vibration-assisted strategy provides a simple and effective approach for improving mass transport in electrochemical sensing systems and demonstrates strong potential for portable environmental monitoring applications.
